title: In 2016 CPEC entered into full implementation Chinese state medi
summary: The China-Pakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC) has achieved significant progress since entering full implementation in 2016, with key projects in energy, infrastructure, and Gwadar Port development enhancing connectivity and creating jobs. Notable milestones include a successful trade convoy through western Pakistan and the first exports from Gwadar Port, while energy initiatives aim to address power shortages. Economists project that CPEC will boost Pakistan's GDP growth and attract foreign investment, positioning it as a transformative project for regional development.

title: State Bank decides maintains interest rate at 575 per
summary: The State Bank of Pakistan decided to maintain the interest rate at 5.75 percent for the upcoming two months. Governor Ashraf Mahmood Wathra highlighted improvements in Pakistan's energy situation, stable oil prices, better private sector loans, and increased income, but noted challenges due to the government not receiving Coalition Support.

title: World Bank announces 57 bln in financing for Afri
summary: The World Bank announced $57 billion in financing for sub-Saharan Africa over the next three fiscal years, including $45 billion from IDA, $8 billion from IFC, and $4 billion from IBRD. This initiative aims to boost development in areas like education, health, and infrastructure, with Germany prioritizing Africa through its G20 'Compact with Africa' partnership. World Bank president Jim Yong Kim is visiting Rwanda and Tanzania to show support for the region.
